title: rhus virens var. australis
intro: Rhus virens var. australis, also known as poison sumac, is a type of flowering plant in the Anacardiaceae family, native to the southeastern United States and parts of the Gulf Coast. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate skin conditions and wounds. The health benefits of poison sumac include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Herbal preparations of Rhus virens var. australis can be made into ointments, salves, and tinctures, which can be applied topically to the affected area.
